scope: Opt-in TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4 Flash 4-layer Hyper-Connection boundary gate.

default_behavior.reason: The retained 8xMI350 performance anchor remains plain-residual unless CANARY_USE_HYPER_CONNECTIONS=true forwards TORCHTITAN_DSV4_USE_HYPER_CONNECTIONS=1.

implementation.block_boundary: hc_pre -> norm/sub-layer -> hc_post around attention and MoE
implementation.model_boundary: token embeddings expand from [B,S,D] to [B,S,4,D], layers operate rank-4, final hc_head collapses to [B,S,D] before norm/lm_head
implementation.parameter_init: normal std=0.02 for hc_fn, ones for scales, zeros for bases
implementation.root_sharding: hc_head_fn, hc_head_scale, and hc_head_base are declared replicated in the root sharding config when HC is enabled

launcher_check_config.command_shape: GPUS_PER_NODE=1 CANARY_EP=1 CANARY_DP_SHARD=1 CANARY_GBS=1 CANARY_SEQ_LEN=16 CANARY_STEPS=1 CANARY_MOE_COMM_BACKEND=standard CANARY_USE_HYPER_CONNECTIONS=true launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh check-config

read: This closes the structural TorchTitan HC-boundary gap that blocked using TorchTitan as an HF/Ascend-like architecture surface.
This is not yet matched-weight HF numerical parity and not a performance run.
The retained 58.71 TFLOPs/GPU 8xMI350 systems anchor remains unchanged because HC is opt-in.
next_actions: Rerun the HF parity preflight so torchtitan_wip_lacks_hc flips false.
Add a small runtime forward/backward smoke only after deciding a reduced HC-enabled config shape that will not disturb retained performance canaries.
Run matched-weight HF-vs-local numerical parity before using TorchTitan HC as a training correctness reference.
